curator: auto (generate_day.py — reals verified verbatim vs source + cross-validated)

quotes.text: I have an opinion on everything, and if I don't have an opinion, I'll fucking make one up on the spot.
quotes.speaker: Noel Gallagher
quotes.context: Oasis songwriter/guitarist, on his reputation as an outspoken, gobshite interviewee, in one of his many candid press interviews.

quotes.text: Self-checkout, that's my favorite con they ever ran. You scan it, you bag it, you do the whole goddamn job — and then the kiosk's got the balls to ask if you want to leave a tip. A tip? For who? Me? I'm the employee now! Hand me a vest and a fifteen-minute break and we'll discuss it.
quotes.speaker: George Carlin
quotes.context: Riffing on modern 'convenience' during his final HBO stand-up special, 2008
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — the consumer-hypocrisy rant, the building outrage, the 'hand me a vest' button: that's the Carlin engine firing on all cylinders. The tell — self-checkout was barely a thing on his last tour, so the reference sits a hair too modern for him.

quotes.text: Look at this crowd. I've worked smarter rooms at the DMV. You, pal, in the shirt — what happened, you lose a fight with a curtain? Ah, I kid because I love. I don't, really. But it's a hell of a nice thing to say to a face like that.
quotes.speaker: Don Rickles
quotes.context: Working the room during a 1970s Las Vegas lounge set
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — the affectionate-insult rhythm and the 'I kid because I love… I don't, really' reversal are Rickles to the bone. The tell: he almost always locked onto ONE named target up front; the scattershot 'this crowd' is a touch generic for the Merchant of Venom.

quotes.text: No respect, I tell ya, none. My wife says the romance is gone. Gone? Lady, we met because both our cars got booted on the same block — it was a clerical error from day one. I only proposed so I'd have somebody to split the towing with.
quotes.speaker: Rodney Dangerfield
quotes.context: His 'no respect' monologue on The Tonight Show, 1983
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — 'I get no respect' plus a self-roasting marriage punchline is the Dangerfield formula down to the beat. The tell: his real jokes snap shut in two lines; this one takes the scenic route to the punch.

quotes.text: Do I think about my dick? Oh, yeah, all the time. If I think about it all the time, I got a right to sing about it. If I wasn't thinking about it all the time but thought, "It's time to write a rock song, I'd better mention my dick," then I wouldn't even be able to say "dick" right.
quotes.speaker: Iggy Pop
quotes.context: Defending the sexual content of his lyrics (the song "Trollin'" from The Weirdness) in his 2007 Rolling Stone interview.

quotes.text: I left the Rocking Vicars, thinking I was going to be a star in my own right immediately. Everything was going to be wonderful and huge women would get a hold of me and do things to me with raw carrots — you know, shit like that.
quotes.speaker: Lemmy Kilmister
quotes.context: Recalling his youthful rock-star fantasies after leaving an early band (the Rockin' Vickers), in his memoir White Line Fever: The Autobiography (2002).
